question_text: What is the role of a product manager?
ideal_answer_summary: A product manager defines product vision roadmap and aligns teams to deliver customer value.
A product roadmap outlines planned features and milestones over time.
MVP is a basic version of product built to validate assumptions with minimal resources.
Product lifecycle describes stages from launch to decline.
User story is a short description of feature from user perspective.
Backlog is prioritized list of product features and tasks.
Sprint is a fixed time period to complete planned tasks.
Stakeholder management ensures alignment and clear communication.
Product vision defines long term purpose and direction.
Market research gathers data about customers and competitors.
Value proposition explains why customers should choose the product.
Competitive analysis evaluates competitor strengths and weaknesses.
User persona represents ideal customer characteristics.
PRD defines product features and technical requirements.
A B testing compares two versions to improve performance.
Churn rate measures customers leaving the product.

ideal_answer_summary: KPIs measure product performance against objectives.
Customer journey maps user interaction with product.
Usability testing evaluates product ease of use.
Agile is iterative development approach with continuous feedback.
Scrum is agile framework with defined roles and ceremonies.
Product market fit occurs when product satisfies strong market demand.
Prioritization ranks features based on impact and effort.
Roadmap prioritization aligns features with strategic goals.
Feedback loop gathers customer input for product improvement.
Wireframe is basic visual layout of product interface.
Prototype is early product model for testing ideas.
Product analytics analyzes user data to improve decisions.
Retention rate measures how many users continue using product.
